title: How To Use Claude's Reflect Dashboard And Learn When It's Time To Touch Grass
content_html:
Break reminders are your friend.

Anthropic

Anthropic's Claude chatbot can be an incredible productivity tool, but like any digital experience, it's possible to overuse it to the point where it starts to negatively affect your life. To help users get a better sense of how much time they're spending with Claude, and nurture a more thoughtful relationship with its technology, Anthropic recently released Reflect, a new dashboard inside of the chatbot's settings menu that's a mix between Spotify Wrapped and Apple's Screen Time tool. In this guide, I'll go over how you can use the tool to put limits on your Claude usage.

How to set break reminders and quiet hours

Igor Bonifacic for Engadget

First, you'll want to access the dashboard and generate your first reflect report. To do so, follow these steps:

  1. Open your browser and navigate to claude.ai.

  2. Click on your name and profile photo. 

  3. Click Settings.

  4. Select Reflect.

Claude will take a moment to generate your report. By default, the chatbot will summarize the last month of your usage, but you can also see the last three, six or 12 months by clicking the toggle at the top.

Once your first report is ready, you'll see a short summary of your conversations with Claude. As of the writing of this article, you can't see the exact amount of time you've spent using Claude. If you click the Time spent tab, the page just says "coming soon." Ryn Linthicum, Anthropic's head of wellbeing policy, told Engadget the reason for that is the company didn't have an internal system for measuring time spent on Claude when it began working on the Reflect dashboard.

In any case, the dashboard gives you two different ways to manage your time on Claude. First, you can set break reminders, which the chatbot will deliver in the form of a nudge after you use it for a set amount of time. After just how much time Claude prompts you to take a break, is up to you. You can set reminders for every 15, 30 or 45 minutes, or every few hours. Separately, you can set quiet hours, which are designed to prevent you from using Claude during certain hours of the day. You can set different hours for each day of the week independently of each other. So, for example, on Monday you can put up a roadblock from 5PM to 8PM, while on Saturday you can set it from 12PM to 4PM.

As with any screen time tool, it's ultimately up to you to honor the usage limits you've set for yourself since you can freely dismiss all of Claude's nudges. If you want to tweak your break reminders, you can do so from the Time and focus section of the settings menu.

Anthropic

One more feature of the dashboard I'll highlight here involves the AI fluency section, which you'll find toward the bottom of the interface. Under this section, Claude will generate recommendations designed to streamline your usage of the chatbot. For example, if Claude finds you frequently re-establish the same or similar context when you go to write a question or request, it will recommend you use its Projects feature to group your prompts together, so that you don't need to repeat yourself so often. In my testing, this tool has helped me use Claude smarter. So I recommend giving some of the tips Claude generates a try.

To help users get a better sense of how much time they're spending with Claude, and nurture a more thoughtful relationship with its technology, Anthropic recently released Reflect, a new dashboard inside of the chatbot's settings menu that's a mix between Spotify Wrapped and Apple's Screen Time tool. In this guide, I'll go over how you can use the tool to put limits on your Claude usage. How to set break reminders and quiet hours Igor Bonifacic for Engadget First, you'll want to access the dashboard and generate your first reflect report. To do so, follow these steps: Open your browser and navigate to claude.ai. Click on your name and profile photo. Click Settings. Select Reflect. Claude will take a moment to generate your report. By default, the chatbot will summarize the last month of your usage, but you can also see the last three, six or 12 months by clicking the toggle at the top. Once your first report is ready, you'll see a short summary of your conversations with Claude. As of the writing of this article, you can't see the exact amount of time you've spent using Claude. If you click the Time spent tab, the page just says "coming soon." Ryn Linthicum, Anthropic's head of wellbeing policy, told Engadget the reason for that is the company didn't have an internal system for measuring time spent on Claude when it began working on the Reflect dashboard. In any case, the dashboard gives you two different ways to manage your time on Claude. First, you can set break reminders, which the chatbot will deliver in the form of a nudge after you use it for a set amount of time. After just how much time Claude prompts you to take a break, is up to you. You can set reminders for every 15, 30 or 45 minutes, or every few hours. Anthropic One more feature of the dashboard I'll highlight here involves the AI fluency section, which you'll find toward the bottom of the interface. Under this section, Claude will generate recommendations designed to streamline your usage of the chatbot. For example, if Claude finds you frequently re-establish the same or similar context when you go to write a question or request, it will recommend you use its Projects feature to group your prompts together, so that you don't need to repeat yourself so often. content_html_zh:
休息提醒是你的朋友。

人择

Anthropic 的 Claude 聊天机器人可以成为令人难以置信的生产力工具，但就像任何数字体验一样，过度使用它可能会对您的生活产生负面影响。 为了帮助用户更好地了解他们与 Claude 相处的时间，并与其技术建立更周到的关系，Anthropic 最近发布了 Reflect，这是聊天机器人设置菜单中的一个新仪表板，它是 Spotify Wrapped 和 Apple 屏幕时间工具的混合体。 在本指南中，我将介绍如何使用该工具来限制 Claude 使用。

如何设置休息提醒和安静时间

Engadget 的伊戈尔·博尼法西克

首先，您需要访问仪表板并生成第一个反映报告。 为此，请按照下列步骤操作：

  1. 打开浏览器并导航至 claude.ai。
  2. 单击您的姓名和个人资料照片。
  3. 单击“设置”。
  4. 选择反映。

克劳德需要一些时间来生成您的报告。 默认情况下，聊天机器人会总结您上个月的使用情况，但您也可以通过单击顶部的切换按钮查看过去 3、6 或 12 个月的情况。

第一份报告准备好后，您将看到与克劳德对话的简短摘要。 在撰写本文时，您无法看到您使用 Claude 所花费的确切时间。 如果您单击“花费的时间”选项卡，该页面只会显示“即将推出”。 Anthropic 的福利政策主管 Ryn Linthicum 告诉 Engadget，原因是该公司在开始开发 Reflect 仪表板时没有一个内部系统来衡量 Claude 花费的时间。

无论如何，仪表板为您提供了两种不同的方式来管理您在 Claude 上的时间。 首先，您可以设置休息提醒，聊天机器人会在您使用设定的时间后以轻推的形式提供提醒。 克劳德提示你休息多久后，取决于你。 您可以设置每 15、30 或 45 分钟或每隔几个小时提醒一次。 另外，您可以设置安静时间，旨在防止您在一天中的某些时段使用克劳德。 您可以为一周中的每一天设置彼此独立的不同时间。 例如，周一您可以在下午 5 点到晚上 8 点设置路障，而周六您可以在中午 12 点到下午 4 点设置路障。

与任何屏幕时间工具一样，最终取决于您是否遵守为自己设置的使用限制，因为您可以自由地忽略克劳德的所有提示。 如果您想调整休息提醒，可以从设置菜单的时间和焦点部分进行操作。

人择

我在这里要强调的仪表板的另一项功能涉及人工智能流畅度部分，您可以在界面底部找到该部分。 在本部分中，Claude 将生成旨在简化您对聊天机器人的使用的建议。 例如，如果 Claude 发现您在编写问题或请求时经常重新建立相同或相似的上下文，它会建议您使用其项目功能将提示分组在一起，这样您就不需要经常重复自己。 在我的测试中，这个工具帮助我更聪明地使用 Claude。 所以我建议尝试一下克劳德提出的一些技巧。
